summary.business_type: Free utility SaaS / content-tool hybrid (time & date calculators, clocks, novelty games) — no e-commerce, no local/brick-and-mortar signals
top_findings: Every canonical URL, OG URL, sitemap entry, and JSON-LD url field points at the apex domain (thegodoftime.com), which itself 308-redirects to www.thegodoftime.com — confirmed independently by 5 of 9 audit agents (technical, schema, sitemap, geo, backlinks) as the single highest-confidence issue on the site
/tools, /clocks, and /realms — three of the site's four top-level hub pages — have no canonical tag at all

Click Per Second Test page's FAQ and WebApplication schema claim 'auto clicker detection' and 'live percentile comparison' features that do not exist in CpsTester.tsx — a factual accuracy defect live on the site right now
No sitewide Organization/WebSite schema and no llms.txt exist — the site has no structured brand-entity signal for either traditional SERP knowledge panels or AI/LLM retrieval

Homepage and World Clock measure Poor LCP (7.8s, mobile lab); Age Calculator and Countdown Timer measure severe Total Blocking Time (1.2-1.8s) — all four driven by main-thread JS/hydration cost, not network
quick_wins: Fix the apex→www domain mismatch in 3 files (app/layout.tsx metadataBase, app/sitemap.ts BASE_URL, app/robots.ts sitemap field) — highest leverage, lowest effort fix on the whole audit
Add alternates.canonical to app/tools/page.tsx, app/clocks/page.tsx, app/realms/page.tsx (3-line fix each, same pattern already used on app/games/page.tsx)
Regenerate icon-192.png/icon-512.png — currently byte-identical 562KB mislabeled files, confirmed as the single largest resource loaded on the homepage
Correct or remove the CPS-test page's false auto-clicker-detection / percentile-comparison claims
Delete the confirmed-dead app/tools/[slug]/page.tsx placeholder route (101/101 registry tools already have dedicated pages; this route is unreachable)

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: Redirect hygiene is otherwise clean: single-hop apex→www 308, trailing/double-slash normalization, ~46 legacy slugs correctly redirected via next.config.ts
Fully server-rendered content and internal links — no "use client" in any app/**/page.tsx, confirmed 101 tool links present in raw pre-JS /tools HTML
Structured data present and correctly typed (WebApplication, BreadcrumbList, FAQPage) on tool and realm pages
Dynamically generated 171-URL sitemap driven by the same content registries as routing — new pages auto-get sitemap coverage
Lightweight footprint: no next/image/ beyond small icons, self-hosted fonts, correct true-404 handling, unrestrictive mobile viewport

findings.title: Canonical/OG/sitemap all point to apex domain that 308-redirects to www
findings.description: metadataBase (app/layout.tsx), sitemap BASE_URL (app/sitemap.ts), and the robots.ts sitemap field all hardcode https://thegodoftime.com, but that host 308-redirects to https://www.thegodoftime.com — the only host that serves 200s. Every canonical tag, OG URL, and all 171 sitemap URLs point to a redirecting URL.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on www everywhere (metadataBase, sitemap BASE_URL, robots.ts sitemap field); centralize into one exported SITE_URL constant so it can't drift again.

findings.title: /tools, /clocks, /realms hub pages have no canonical tag
findings.description: Live checks confirm /tools, /clocks, /realms render no <link rel="canonical">, while / and /games do. These are 3 of the site's 4 top-level hub pages.
findings.recommendation: Add alternates: { canonical: "/tools" } (etc.) to each hub page's metadata export, matching app/games/page.tsx's existing pattern.

findings.title: No security response headers beyond automatic HSTS
findings.description: Missing X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options/CSP frame-ancestors, Content-Security-Policy, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy. No headers() in next.config.ts, no middleware.ts, no vercel.json.
findings.recommendation: Add a headers() function in next.config.ts (nosniff, SAMEORIGIN, strict-origin-when-cross-origin, scoped Permissions-Policy). Defer full CSP until the inline GA4 bootstrap script is nonce'd/externalized.

findings.title: No IndexNow protocol implementation
findings.description: No IndexNow key file or push integration exists despite daily-refreshing content (/games) and a growing set of pages.
findings.recommendation: Generate an IndexNow key, host at public/<key>.txt, ping api.indexnow.org when registries change.

findings.title: robots.txt Sitemap directive points to redirecting apex host
findings.description: robots.txt declares Sitemap: https://thegodoftime.com/sitemap.xml, itself 308-redirecting — an unnecessary hop.
findings.recommendation: Fixed by the same code change as the top canonicalization finding.

findings.title: ads.txt returns 404
findings.description: Expected pre-monetization state — no AdSense publisher ID exists yet, so no placeholder file was created.
findings.recommendation: Add ads.txt once AdSense approval issues a publisher ID.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Hand-written, factually specific tool content (Naegele's Rule math, IVF transfer offsets, pay-differential benchmarks) rather than generic AI boilerplate
All 101 tool pages emit valid FAQPage JSON-LD with 3-8 genuine Q&A pairs each on a consistent intro → how-to → sections → FAQ hierarchy
Registry/folder integrity is excellent: 101/101 registry tools have dedicated pages; the feared [slug] fallback is confirmed unreachable dead code, not live thin content
Transparent Privacy Policy naming Google Analytics/AdSense with working opt-out links
Readability is consistent (~grade 9-11), no keyword stuffing observed

findings.title: /realms/absurd-clocks is a live near-duplicate of /realms/watch-paint-dry
findings.description: Reachable via the realms [slug] catch-all, absurd-clocks renders the identical PaintDrySimulator component but the unique SEO content already written for it in realmsRegistry.ts is never rendered by the catch-all route — that content is dead data.
findings.recommendation: Give absurd-clocks its own static page rendering its already-written registry SEO content, or 301-redirect it to watch-paint-dry and remove from generateStaticParams.

findings.title: No author/organization/named-entity E-E-A-T signal anywhere
findings.description: No Organization/Person schema, no named team, no visible 'not medical/legal advice' disclaimer on individual YMYL-adjacent tool pages (pregnancy, ovulation, medication, legal-deadline calculators) despite Terms covering it site-wide.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'who makes this' section to /about, Organization JSON-LD site-wide, and visible per-page disclaimers on YMYL-adjacent calculators.

findings.title: Support email is a known placeholder per source TODO
findings.description: Contact page hardcodes support@thegodoftime.com behind a TODO noting it should be replaced with a real form once the domain/email are fully set up, while user-facing copy claims 'we read every message.'
findings.recommendation: Confirm the inbox is live/monitored before treating Contact as production-ready, or implement the referenced form integration.

findings.title: ~70% of tool pages are under ~400 words, well below the site's own benchmark pages
findings.description: Content ranges from ~147 words (vaccination-tracker) to ~866 words (time-card-calculator); ~71/101 pages lack the richer H2 sections block the site's best pages use.
findings.recommendation: Backfill the sections block used on shift-differential-calculator/pregnancy-due-date-calculator to the thinnest ~40-50 pages, prioritizing high-value and YMYL-adjacent tools first.

findings.title: No content freshness / last-updated signals
findings.description: No dateModified/datePublished in any JSON-LD, no visible last-updated date anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Add dateModified to ToolPageData and surface a reviewed date in schema/UI.

findings.title: [slug] placeholder route confirmed unreachable dead code
findings.description: Verified via generateStaticParams/folder cross-reference and a live 404 test — Next.js route precedence makes it permanently unreachable while registry/folder stay in sync.
findings.recommendation: Delete app/tools/[slug]/page.tsx as cleanup, or add a build-time assertion guaranteeing sync.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Title tags and meta descriptions are consistently unique, keyword-led, and within reasonable length across every page checked this audit and throughout the site's build history
Single H1 per page with logical H2/H3 hierarchy, verified consistently on sampled tool/clock/realm pages
Internal linking is dense and relevant (breadcrumbs, related-tool rails, cross-links between clocks/tools/realms)

findings.title: 3 of 4 top-level hub pages missing canonical tags
findings.description: See Technical SEO — /tools, /clocks, /realms render no canonical tag.
findings.recommendation: See Technical SEO recommendation.

findings.title: Click Per Second Test page content overclaims unbuilt features
findings.description: The WebApplication featureList and an on-page FAQ claim auto-clicker detection (>16 CPS flagging) and 'percentile comparison against other testers.' Neither exists in components/clocks/experiences/CpsTester.tsx — no detection logic exists, and the score panel is a static hardcoded rank-tier lookup, not a live percentile.
findings.recommendation: Either implement the two claimed features (a CPS threshold flag is trivial; percentile needs at least a static distribution table) or rewrite the schema/FAQ copy to only describe what the component actually does.

findings.title: Comparison-shopper differentiator claims buried in prose instead of shown as scannable proof
findings.description: Real competitive differentiators (e.g. World Clock's multi-city-at-once view) are embedded mid-paragraph rather than presented as a scannable checklist for the comparison-shopping persona clearly present in multi-competitor SERPs.
findings.recommendation: Pull 2-3 of these claims into a small visual 'why this tool' checklist near the top of the SEO content zone on high-competition pages.

findings.title: World Clock ships with zero city times in server-rendered HTML
findings.description: WorldClock.tsx seeds city state as useState([]), populated only in a mount useEffect, so crawlers and first-paint see an empty widget with no content-parity against competitors' static reference tables (timeanddate.com, worldclock.com).
findings.recommendation: Server-render a default table of ~10-15 major city times as real HTML for first-paint/crawl safety and competitive content parity.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: BreadcrumbList schema applied almost universally across clocks, tools, realms, and games via shared layout components
FAQPage generation is templatized and DRY across all three content-type SEO components, only rendering when an FAQ array is populated
No deprecated schema types found anywhere despite many step-based tools that would tempt a HowTo implementation
Existing WebApplication blocks are well-formed (correct @context, offers, applicationCategory, featureList) with no placeholder text in any sampled block

findings.title: No sitewide Organization or WebSite schema exists anywhere
findings.description: Zero matches for Organization/WebSite @type across the entire codebase. No structured entity/brand signal (name, logo, sameAs); not eligible for a Sitelinks Search Box.
findings.recommendation: Add a single sitewide Organization + WebSite schema rendered once at the root with name, url, logo, description.

findings.title: All JSON-LD url/item values use the redirecting apex domain
findings.description: Same root cause as the Technical SEO canonicalization finding, independently confirmed in every hand-written and auto-generated schema block.
findings.recommendation: Fixed by the same centralized SITE_URL constant fix.

findings.title: Only ~25% of tool pages (25/102) have WebApplication schema
findings.description: WebApplication was added ad hoc over time rather than being part of the shared ToolPageTemplate pattern.
findings.recommendation: Move WebApplication generation into ToolSEOContent.tsx/ToolPageTemplate itself, deriving fields from lib/tools/data/<slug>.ts.

findings.title: Only 4 of 25 built realm pages have WebApplication schema
findings.description: Same ad hoc pattern as tools, mirrored in the realms section.
findings.recommendation: Lift WebApplication generation into RealmLayout.tsx using existing realmsRegistry.ts data.

findings.title: global-shift-overlap clock page has zero structured data beyond BreadcrumbList
findings.description: The only outlier among 32 built clock pages — no WebApplication, no FAQPage.
findings.recommendation: Add WebApplication schema and wire a populated FAQ array through ClockSEOContent, matching sibling pages.

findings.title: Orphaned empty directory: app/clocks/split-flap-planner/
findings.description: No page.tsx, no registry entry — a work-in-progress scaffold, not a live 404.
findings.recommendation: Build it out or delete the directory.

categories.name: Performance (Core Web Vitals)
what_works: TTFB is genuinely good site-wide (70-194ms measured via real Lighthouse runs on 4 sampled pages) — Vercel hosting is not a bottleneck
Font loading is well-implemented: next/font self-hosts all 4 families, zero font-display issues flagged
CLS is essentially solved: 3 of 4 pages measured exactly 0, the 4th (Age Calculator) a comfortable 0.084
Third-party script hygiene is good: GA loads via correct afterInteractive strategy; AdSense loader confirmed correctly environment-gated and absent from every trace
DOM size well controlled (242-370 elements) on all sampled pages

findings.title: Homepage and World Clock LCP is Poor (7.8s mobile lab)
findings.description: Measured via real Lighthouse runs. Driven by 1.6s+ Script Evaluation and 1.0-1.1s Style & Layout work on main thread before the LCP text node paints — not a network issue (TTFB is good).
findings.recommendation: Defer non-critical client-side hydration on the above-the-fold path; keep the LCP element and ancestors in Server Components where possible.

findings.title: Age Calculator and Countdown Timer have severe Total Blocking Time (1.2-1.8s)
findings.description: TBT measured 1,767ms and 1,222ms respectively. Single tasks of 640ms/498ms from the page bundle, plus recurring tasks from a shared 72KB chunk (37.6% unused) and gtag.js during hydration.
findings.recommendation: Code-split the shared vendor/app chunk; break large hydration tasks into smaller units via scheduler.yield()/requestIdleCallback.

findings.title: icon-192.png and icon-512.png are identical, mislabeled, oversized (562KB each)
findings.description: Both are byte-for-byte identical 1024x1024 baseline JPEGs saved with a .png extension despite manifest.json declaring 192x192/512x512 PNGs. Confirmed as the single largest resource fetched on the homepage — bigger than all JS combined.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate real, correctly-sized/encoded icon assets matching manifest.json's declarations.

findings.title: World Clock's per-second tick does non-memoized Intl.DateTimeFormat work for every pinned city
findings.description: setInterval fires every 1000ms, instantiating 2-3 new Intl.DateTimeFormat objects per pinned clock (up to 10) every tick, none memoized. Real recurring main-thread cost not fully captured by a single load trace.
findings.recommendation: Cache Intl.DateTimeFormat instances per timezone in a reused Map; reuse one shared AudioContext instead of creating one per split-flap character flip.

findings.title: gtag.js is deferred correctly but still meaningfully sized/partially unused
findings.description: 163.7KB transferred, 41.98% (67.4KB) unused per Lighthouse; appears in long-tasks on every page.
findings.recommendation: Consider strategy=lazyOnload or manually delaying injection by 2-3s since GA events queue via dataLayer.push.

findings.title: Global CSS bundle (~27KB) is render-blocking on every page
findings.description: Flagged on all 4 pages tested, contributing an estimated 150-166ms blocking duration each.
findings.recommendation: Audit for unused CSS per-route; lower priority than JS/LCP findings.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness (GEO)
what_works: Genuinely citable FAQ content at scale: hand-written, specific, number-anchored answers correctly wrapped in FAQPage JSON-LD, confirmed present in raw pre-JS HTML
Fully open robots.txt with no blocks on GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, or OAI-SearchBot
Solid SSR foundation confirmed via X-Nextjs-Prerender header — even "use client" pages deliver full text and JSON-LD in initial HTML with no JS-execution dependency for crawlers
Clear, self-contained entity framing on the About page describing what the site is in plain extractable prose

findings.title: No llms.txt exists
findings.description: public/llms.txt returns 404. 171 sitemap URLs across 4 content types have no purpose-built index for LLM retrieval.
findings.recommendation: Add public/llms.txt with a site description plus grouped Markdown link lists per section, generated at build time from the existing registries so it never drifts out of sync.

findings.title: No sitewide Organization/WebSite schema and zero cross-platform brand signals
findings.description: No sameAs array anywhere in the codebase; Footer has zero outbound social links. YouTube and Reddit/Wikipedia mentions correlate most strongly with AI citation.
findings.recommendation: Add sitewide Organization/WebSite JSON-LD with a sameAs array; prioritize establishing at least one off-site channel over further backlink building.

findings.title: No authorship, content dates, or freshness signals anywhere
findings.description: Zero <lastmod> tags across all 171 sitemap URLs; no datePublished/dateModified metadata despite YMYL-adjacent content.
findings.recommendation: Add <lastmod> to sitemap entries, dateModified to WebApplication schema, and a sitewide methodology note referencing named formulas already used in FAQ copy.

findings.title: Near-zero multi-modal content
findings.description: No images/video/iframes inside SEO content components; no per-page OG image generation — every page falls back to the sitewide icon.svg.
findings.recommendation: Add per-page dynamic OG images (Next.js ImageResponse) as a low-effort first step.

findings.title: robots.txt has no explicit AI-crawler policy and its sitemap URL forces a redirect
findings.description: Functionally open but undocumented; Sitemap: line points at the redirecting apex domain.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit documented Allow rules for major AI crawlers; fix the Sitemap: line as part of the centralized domain fix.

categories.name: Images
what_works: No broken alt-text surface area to speak of, since the site deliberately uses almost no photographic/illustrative content — mostly SVG icons and emoji

findings.title: Primary PWA icons are broken: identical, mislabeled, 562KB each
findings.description: See Performance category — confirmed the single largest resource on the homepage.
findings.recommendation: Regenerate correctly-sized/encoded icon assets.

findings.title: No per-page Open Graph images
findings.description: Every page shares one sitewide icon.svg as its social preview image, regardless of content — a missed differentiation/CTR opportunity on social shares.
findings.recommendation: Generate per-page or per-category OG images via Next.js ImageResponse.

findings.title: Near-zero illustrative/explanatory imagery site-wide
findings.description: No diagrams, screenshots, or explanatory visuals anywhere in tool/clock content, flagged independently by the GEO audit as a multi-modal content gap.
findings.recommendation: Longer-term: add explanatory diagrams with alt text to major tool pages.

categories.name: Sitemap Structure (supplementary)
what_works: Fully dynamic and registry-driven (app/sitemap.ts) — zero manual maintenance, auto-updates with the registries
isExistingTool dedup logic verified correct live: zero duplicate URLs across all 171 entries
Registry-to-route arithmetic reconciles exactly (9 static + 32 clocks + 101 tools + 26 realms + 3 games = 171) with zero orphaned page.tsx files
Valid XML throughout, well under the 50,000-URL limit, correctly omits deprecated priority/changefreq tags

findings.title: Every sitemap URL points to the redirecting apex domain
findings.description: Same root cause as the Technical SEO finding, independently reconfirmed here with live 200-vs-308 verification.
findings.recommendation: Same centralized SITE_URL fix.

findings.title: No lastmod field on any entry
findings.description: Deliberate and reasonable given no real per-page timestamp data exists — but a missed weak recrawl signal.
findings.recommendation: Wire in a genuine lastUpdated field if/when registries gain one. Do not fabricate fake dates.

categories.name: Visual & Mobile (supplementary)
what_works: Strong above-the-fold desktop and mobile homepage: H1, subhead, CTAs, and live widget visible without scrolling
Well-built mobile navigation overlay reproducing the full categorized mega-menu
Consistent neo-brutalist dark theme and typography hierarchy across all page types tested
Ad placeholders are clearly labeled, non-intrusive, never overlap calculator inputs/results
Zero console errors and zero horizontal overflow across all pages tested at desktop and mobile viewports

findings.title: World Clock card controls fail minimum touch-target size on mobile
findings.description: Per-clock prev/next/remove icon buttons measure 22x22px (guideline ~44-48px); the remove (✕) button sits directly beside navigation with no spacing buffer, risking accidental deletion.
findings.recommendation: Increase tappable hit area to at least 44x44px via padding; add spacing/confirm step around the remove action.

findings.title: Floating ad rails render flush against the browser edge at ~1600px viewport width
findings.description: At the common 1600x900 laptop resolution, both ad boxes touch the viewport edge with 0px margin, unlike the ~170px gutter at 1920px.
findings.recommendation: Add a guaranteed minimum outer margin or raise the hide breakpoint to ~1650px.

findings.title: Mobile breadcrumb/H1 truncation on long tool names
findings.description: On narrow viewports, breadcrumbs and H1s for long tool names truncate/wrap awkwardly, pushing the calculator further down the fold.
findings.recommendation: Drop the current-page breadcrumb crumb on mobile or apply graceful ellipsis; consider shorter H1s for long-named tools.

findings.title: Live stat widget awkwardly bisected by the fold on mobile homepage
findings.description: The live clock/stats card is only ~10% visible at the bottom edge at 375x812, looking like an accidental crop.
findings.recommendation: Reposition above the CTA stack as a trust signal, or push fully below the fold with adequate margin.

categories.name: Search Experience (SXO, supplementary)
what_works: Tool-first layout matches the dominant SERP pattern for transactional/utility queries — every top competitor also puts the calculator above the fold before prose
Time Card Calculator's content depth and default grid closely mirror category leaders, including specific FLSA rules
Pregnancy Due Date Calculator correctly covers all three real calculation methods with appropriate medical hedging
Click Per Second Test's double-click hardware diagnostic mode is a genuine working differentiator most competitors lack

findings.title: YMYL E-E-A-T signals missing on pregnancy-due-date-calculator
findings.description: Top SERP results (APA, NHS, UpToDate, hospitals) all carry reviewer credentials, last-revised dates, and citation networks. This page has good depth but zero author/reviewedBy/dateModified and no outbound authoritative citations, for a query bordering on medical advice.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'reviewed against ACOG/NHS guidelines' line with a real date, dateModified/author in schema, and 1-2 outbound citations to ACOG/NHS/Mayo. Extend to the whole Health & Lifecycle group.

findings.title: Click-per-second-test schema/FAQ claim features that don't exist in the code
findings.description: Cross-referenced with the On-Page SEO finding above.
findings.recommendation: See On-Page SEO recommendation.

findings.title: World Clock renders empty on first paint and lacks a static reference table
findings.description: Cross-referenced with the On-Page SEO finding above.
findings.recommendation: See On-Page SEO recommendation.

findings.title: Time Card Calculator has no export/print, the one feature every top competitor offers
findings.description: Zero print/export/CSV/download functionality anywhere on the site, while category leaders offer PDF/CSV export or printable reports.
findings.recommendation: Add a lightweight print stylesheet or 'copy summary' action to HR/Payroll tools.

findings.title: CPS test has the mechanics of a challenge but not the social hook the intent implies
findings.description: "Click per second test" intent is social/competitive per SERP language, but the world-record comparison only exists as FAQ prose, never surfaced live next to the user's own score, and there's no share action.
findings.recommendation: Surface 'You: X CPS — World record: 14.1 CPS' live in the results panel and add a one-tap share/copy action.

categories.name: Backlinks (supplementary)
categories.weight: not in core weighting — supplementary, expected baseline for a brand-new site
what_works: No technical blockers to future backlink accumulation: robots.txt open, sitemap live and well-formed
No evidence anywhere of a toxic, spammy, or penalized domain history — the one legacy Wayback snapshot (2018 robots.txt) is benign
Genuinely link-worthy content foundation: 100+ free, working interactive calculators/clocks/games is exactly the content type that attracts organic listicle/directory/forum links

findings.title: Domain has zero footprint in Common Crawl's index
findings.description: Expected for site age (weeks-old content, no outreach run yet) — not a defect.
findings.recommendation: Submit the sitemap to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools; pursue scoped link-building (Product Hunt launch, free-tool directories, targeted per-calculator subreddit posts, listicle outreach).

findings.title: Zero referring domains found via any free public source
findings.description: Consistent zero-backlink baseline across Common Crawl, Wayback Machine, and Hacker News search.
findings.recommendation: Treat as a starting line, not a problem.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Centralize the domain: update metadataBase (app/layout.tsx), BASE_URL (app/sitemap.ts), and the sitemap field (app/robots.ts) to https://www.thegodoftime.com — fixes canonical tags, OG URLs, sitemap URLs, and JSON-LD url fields in one coordinated change (confirmed by 5 of 9 agents as the top issue)
Add alternates.canonical to app/tools/page.tsx, app/clocks/page.tsx, app/realms/page.tsx
Fix or remove the false 'auto-clicker detection' / 'percentile comparison' claims on the Click Per Second Test page's FAQ and WebApplication schema
Add real author/reviewed-date/citation signals to pregnancy-due-date-calculator specifically (highest YMYL exposure on the site)

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add sitewide Organization + WebSite JSON-LD schema with a sameAs array
Add public/llms.txt generated from the existing content registries
Regenerate icon-192.png and icon-512.png (currently identical mislabeled 562KB files, largest resource on the homepage)
Add security headers via next.config.ts headers() (nosniff, frame-ancestors, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
Server-render a default city-times table on World Clock instead of an empty first paint
Investigate and reduce LCP on Home/World Clock and TBT on Age Calculator/Countdown Timer (code-split the shared 72KB chunk, defer non-critical hydration)
Memoize Intl.DateTimeFormat instances in WorldClock.tsx's per-second tick; reuse one AudioContext instead of creating one per split-flap flip
Fix World Clock's mobile touch targets (22px → 44px minimum) and add spacing around the remove button
Fix the ad-rail edge-flush layout at ~1600px viewport width

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Backfill the H2 'sections' content block to the ~40-50 thinnest tool pages (currently under ~400 words), prioritizing YMYL-adjacent tools
Add WebApplication schema to the ~77 tool pages and ~21 realm pages currently missing it, by lifting it into the shared template components
Fix /realms/absurd-clocks to render its own unique registry content instead of duplicating watch-paint-dry
Add dateModified/lastmod signals sitewide (sitemap entries + schema)
Add per-page/per-category Open Graph images
Confirm the support@thegodoftime.com inbox is actually live and monitored, or ship the planned support form
Add print/export functionality to Time Card Calculator and related HR/Payroll tools
Delete the confirmed-dead app/tools/[slug]/page.tsx placeholder route

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Set up Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools, submit the sitemap
Begin scoped link-building appropriate to a new free-tool site: Product Hunt launch, free-tool directories, relevant per-calculator subreddit posts, 'best free tools' listicle outreach
Establish at least one off-site brand channel (YouTube demo clips or genuine Reddit participation) to support AI citation signals
Re-run this audit's Performance category once AdSense goes live with a real publisher ID (ad rail containers should be pre-sized to the eventual ad slot dimensions to avoid new CLS)
Add an IndexNow key and push integration
